output.code:
# Load the user's text file
path = "/mnt/data/file-McvUkwcJuibWHhdW29P2ve-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:3000]

# Find all quotes
quote_positions = [i for i, ch in enumerate(text) if ch == '"']
len(quote_positions), quote_positions[:20], quote_positions[-5:]

segments = []
for i in range(0, len(quote_positions)-1, 2):
    start = quote_positions[i]+1
    end = quote_positions[i+1]
    segments.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
len(segments), segments[0][2][:50], segments[1][2][:50]

# Add the last unmatched segment
start_last = quote_positions[-1] + 1
end_last = len(text)
segments.append((start_last, end_last, text[start_last:end_last]))
len(segments), segments[-1][2]

queries: What is the text of Quran Surah As-Saff 61:10 and 61:11 in Arabic Othmani?
يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ تِجَارَةٍ تُنجِيكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
What is the Arabic of 61:10 as-Saff?
نص الآية يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ تِجَارَةٍ تُنجِيكُم
ما نص الآية 61:10 بالعربية رسم عثماني؟

output.code: import os, glob, json, textwrap, sys, re, itertools, math, pathlib, pprint

# Try to locate candidate files in /mnt/data
files = glob.glob("/mnt/data/*")
files[:50], len(files)

# Build citation spans
spans = []

# helper to add a span
def add_span(start, end, citation_type, span_text):
    spans.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": citation_type
    })

# The first quoted segment is the Quran passage
q_start, q_end, q_text = segments[0]
q_text, q_start, q_end

# Compute spans for the two ayahs
first_aya_start = q_start
first_aya_end = q_start + sep_idx
first_aya_text = text[first_aya_start:first_aya_end]

second_aya_start = q_start + sep_idx + 3
second_aya_end = q_end
second_aya_text = text[second_aya_start:second_aya_end]

first_aya_text, second_aya_text[:20], first_aya_start, first_aya_end, second_aya_start, second_aya_end

# Add the two Quran spans
add_span(first_aya_start, first_aya_end, "Aya", first_aya_text)
add_span(second_aya_start, second_aya_end, "Aya", second_aya_text)

# Add hadith spans for each subsequent segment (1..11) and final (12)
for i in range(1, len(segments)):
    start, end, seg_text = segments[i]
    add_span(start, end, "Hadeeth", seg_text)

len(spans)
[(i, segments[i][0], segments[i][1]) for i in range(len(segments))], len(segments)

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
